Location : westus2 Id : /subscriptions/mySubs/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/MyAnfAccount Name : MyAnfAccount Type : Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts Tags : ProvisioningState : Succeeded

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new Azure NetApp Files (ANF) volume.
The New-AzNetAppFilesVolume cmdlet creates an ANF volume.

While auto splitting the short term clone volume, if the parent pool does not have enough space to accommodate the volume after split, it will be automatically resized, which will lead to increased billing. To accept capacity pool size auto grow and create a short term clone volume, set the property as accepted
The name of the ANF account
Specifies whether the volume is enabled for Azure VMware Solution (AVS) datastore purpose (Enabled, Disabled)
A hashtable array which represents the backup object
Backup ID. UUID v4 or resource identifier used to identify the Backup
Pool Resource Id used in case of creating a volume through volume group.
Specifies whether Cool Access(tiering) is enabled for the volume (default false).

CoolAccessRetrievalPolicy determines the data retrieval behavior from the cool tier to standard storage based on the read pattern for cool access enabled volumes. The possible values for this field are: Default - Data will be pulled from cool tier to standard storage on random reads. This policy is the default. OnRead - All client-driven data read is pulled from cool tier to standard storage on both sequential and random reads. Never - No client-driven data is pulled from cool tier to standard storage.

CoolAccessTieringPolicy determines which cold data blocks are moved to cool tier. The possible values for this field are: Auto - Moves cold user data blocks in both the Snapshot copies and the active file system to the cool tier tier. This policy is the default. SnapshotOnly - Moves user data blocks of the Volume Snapshot copies that are not associated with the active file system to the cool tier.
Specifies the number of days after which data that is not accessed by clients will be tiered (minimum 7, maximum 63).
A unique file path for the volume
Default group quota for volume in KiBs. If isDefaultQuotaEnabled is set, the minimum value of 4 KiBs applies.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Default user quota for volume in KiBs. If isDefaultQuotaEnabled is set, the minimum value of 4 KiBs applies.
If enabled (true) the snapshot the volume was created from will be automatically deleted after the volume create operation has finished. Defaults to false
Flag indicating whether subvolume operations are enabled on the volume (Enabled, Disabled)
Source of key used to encrypt data in volume. Applicable if NetApp account has encryption.keySource = 'Microsoft.KeyVault'. Possible values are: 'Microsoft.NetApp, Microsoft.KeyVault'
A hashtable array which represents the export policy
Specifies if default quota is enabled for the volume
Specifies whether volume is a Large Volume or Regular Volume. Defaults to false
Describe if a volume is Kerberos Enabled
The resource ID of private endpoint for KeyVault. It must reside in the same VNET as the volume. Only applicable if encryptionKeySource = 'Microsoft.KeyVault'
Specifies whether LDAP is enabled or not for a given NFS volume.
The location of the resource
The name of the ANF volume
Basic network, or Standard features available to the volume (Basic, Standard).
Application specific placement rules for the particular volume.
The name of the ANF pool
A hashtable array which represents the export policy
Proximity placement group associated with the volume.
A hashtable array which represents the replication object
The resource group of the ANF account
The security style of volume. Possible values include: 'ntfs', 'unix'
The service level of the ANF volume
Enables access based enumeration share property for SMB Shares. Only applicable for SMB/DualProtocol volume
Enables continuously available share property for SMB volume. Only applicable for SMB volume.
Enables encryption for in-flight smb3 data. Only applicable for SMB/DualProtocol volume.
Enables non browsable property for SMB Shares. Only applicable for SMB/DualProtocol volume
A hashtable array which represents the snapshot object
If enabled (true) the volume will contain a read-only .snapshot directory which provides access to each of the volume's snapshots (default to true)
Create volume from a snapshot. UUID v4 or resource identifier used to identify the Snapshot
Snapshot Policy ResourceId used to apply a snapshot policy to the volume
The Azure Resource URI for a delegated subnet
A hashtable which represents resource tags
Maximum throughput in Mibps that can be achieved by this volume

UNIX permissions for NFS volume accepted in octal 4 digit format. First digit selects the set user ID(4), set group ID (2) and sticky (1) attributes. Second digit selects permission for the owner of the file: read (4), write (2) and execute (1). Third selects permissions for other users in the same group. the fourth for other users not in the group. 0755 - gives read/write/execute permissions to owner and read/execute to group and other users.
The maximum storage quota allowed for a file system in bytes
Volume spec name is the application specific designation or identifier for the particular volume in a volume group for e.g. data, log.
The type of the ANF volume
A list of Availability Zones
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: --------------- Example 1: Create an ANF volume ---------------
command:example.dev:code:
New-AzNetAppFilesVolume -ResourceGroupName "MyRG" -AccountName "MyAnfAccount" -PoolName "MyAnfPool" -Name "MyAnfVolume" -Location "westus2" -CreationToken "MyAnfVolume" -UsageThreshold 1099511627776 -ServiceLevel "Premium" -SubnetId "/subscriptions/subsId/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/MyVnetName/subnets/MySubNetName"
Location : westus2 Id : /subscriptions/subsId/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/MyAnfAccount/capacityPools/MyAnfPool/volumes/MyAnfVolume Name : MyAnfAccount/MyAnfPool/MyAnfVolume Type : Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/capacityPools/volumes Tags : FileSystemId : 3e2773a7-2a72-d003-0637-1a8b1fa3eaaf CreationToken : MyAnfVolume ServiceLevel : Premium UsageThreshold : 1099511627776 ProvisioningState : Succeeded SubnetId : /subscriptions/f557b96d-2308-4a18-aae1-b8f7e7e70cc7/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/MyVnetName/subnets/default

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates the new ANF volume "MyAnfVolume" within the pool "MyAnfPool".

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Create an ANF volume using NFSv4.1 Protocol Type. Note do not use the IP adddress block shown for -AllowedClient. This is an example IPv4 address blocks for use in documentation.
command:example.dev:code:
$exportPolicyRule = New-AzNetAppFilesExportPolicyRuleObject -RuleIndex 1 -AllowedClient "192.0.2.0/24" -UnixReadWrite -Nfsv41 New-AzNetAppFilesVolume -ResourceGroupName "MyRG" -AccountName "MyAnfAccount" -PoolName "MyAnfPool" -Name "MyAnfVolume" -Location "westus2" -CreationToken "MyAnfVolume" -ProtocolType NFSv4.1 -ExportPolicy $exportPolicyRule -UsageThreshold 1099511627776 -ServiceLevel "Premium" -SubnetId "/subscriptions/subsId/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/MyVnetName/subnets/MySubNetName"
Location : westus2 Id : /subscriptions/subsId/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/MyAnfAccount/capacityPools/MyAnfPool/volumes/MyAnfVolume Name : MyAnfAccount/MyAnfPool/MyAnfVolume Type : Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/capacityPools/volumes Tags : FileSystemId : 3e2773a7-2a72-d003-0637-1a8b1fa3eaaf CreationToken : MyAnfVolume ServiceLevel : Premium UsageThreshold : 1099511627776 ProvisioningState : Succeeded SubnetId : /subscriptions/f557b96d-2308-4a18-aae1-b8f7e7e70cc7/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/MyVnetName/subnets/default

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates the new ANF volume "MyAnfVolume" within the pool "MyAnfPool" using NFSv4.1 protocol including the required ExportPolicy.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new Azure NetApp Files (ANF) VolumeGroup along with requisite volumes. Creating volume group will create all the volumes specified in request body implicitly. Once volumes are created using volume group, those will be treated as regular volumes thereafter.
The New-AzNetAppFilesVolumeGroup cmdlet creates an ANF VolumeGroup.
The name of the ANF account
Application specific identifier, default SAP System ID SH1
Application Type, default SAP-HANA
A hashtable array which represents the protocol types for Data Backup/Log Backup volumes default NFSv4.1, for other volume types nfsv4.1 will be used.
Capacity overhead %, Additional quota reserved for snapshots during best-practice sizing of data volume, default 50
Specify throughput in MiB/s. If omitted DataBackupPerformance will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing throughput.
Specify capacity (in GiB). If omitted DataSize will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing size.
Specify throughput in MiB/s. If omitted DataPerformance will be autocalculated or specify and integer value representing throughput.
Specify capacity (in GiB). If omitted DataSize will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing size.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Create volume groups for DR, using ANF Cross Region Replication, scenario allows volumes to be replicated between different regions using SnapMirror

Source of key used to encrypt data in volume. Applicable if NetApp account has encryption.keySource = 'Microsoft.KeyVault'. Possible values are: 'Microsoft.NetApp, Microsoft.KeyVault'. To create a volume using customer-managed keys use 'Microsoft.KeyVault' note then you must set -NetworkFeature to Standard.
A hashtable array which represents the export policy, which should be common to all volumes.
Application specific placement rules for the volume group
Group Description, example Primary for SH1-{HostId} (default)
HANA System Replication (HSR): Replication between the same SID instance on hosts in the same region, or different regions. This could be Scale-Up or Scale-Out configurations.
Number of SAP HANA hosts. Total Number of SAP HANA hosts for single or multiplehost scenarios. Defaults to 50 for single-host setups. Currently at max 3 nodes can be configured.
The resource ID of private endpoint for KeyVault. It must reside in the same VNET as the volume. Only applicable if encryptionKeySource = 'Microsoft.KeyVault'
The location of the resource
Specify throughput in MiB/s. If omitted LogBackupPerformance will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing throughput.
Specify capacity (in GiB). If omitted DataSize will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing size.
Specify throughput in MiB/s. If omitted LogPerformance will be autocalculated or specify and integer value representing throughput.
Specify capacity (in GiB). If omitted LogSize will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing size.

The name of the ANF VolumeGroup, example SAP-HANA-SH00001. Defaults to SAP-HANA-{HostId}, where the {HostId} pattern in the name will be replaced by a 5 digit host ID that begins at 1 for the Single-host and counts up for the subsequent Multiple-host, host
Basic network, or Standard features available to the volume (Basic, Standard).
Basic network, or Standard features available to the volume (Basic, Standard).
SAP node memory (GiB), Memory on SAP compute host
Default capacity pool for volumes, use a manual QoS type capacity pool
All volume names will be prefixed with the given text. The default values for prefix text depends on system role. For PRIMARY it will be empty and HA it will be "HA - "
Default proximity placement group, for data, log, and if present the shared volume, in all volume groups. Specifies that the data, log, and shared volumes are to be created close to the VMs
The resource group of the ANF VolumeGroup
Specify throughput in MiB/s. If omitted SharedPerformance will be autocalculated or specify and integer value representing throughput.
Specify capacity (in GiB). If omitted SharedSize will be autocalculated or specify an integer value representing size.
Starting SAP HANA Host ID. Host ID 1 indicates Master Host. Shared, Data Backup and Log Backup volumes are only provisioned for Master Host i.e. HostID == 1.1
Default delegated subnet, for all volume groups
Unique Oracle system ID, default SAP System ID SH1 , default Oracle System Id (OID)ORA1
The role of the system, Primary SAP system, HANA System Replication(HSR) or DataRecovery destination for ANF Cross-region replication (CRR)
A hashtable which represents resource tags
Default virtual network, for all volume groups
A list of Availability Zones

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates the new "PRIMARY" ANF VolumeGroup "MyAnfVolumeGroup" within the Account "MyAnfAccount" using the proximityPlacementGroup "MyPPGResourceId", the vnet "MyAnfVnet", and NodeMemory of 100
This command creates the new "PRIMARY" ANF VolumeGroup "MyAnfVolumeGroup" within the Account "MyAnfAccount" using Zone 1, the vnet "MyAnfVnet", and NodeMemory of 100

Domain Users to be added to the Built-in Administrators Active Directory group. A list of unique usernames without domain specifier.

kdc server IP addresses for the active directory machine. This optional parameter is used only while creating kerberos volume.

LDAP Search scope options.
When LDAP over SSL/TLS is enabled, Specifies whether or not the LDAP traffic needs to be signed.
The Organizational Unit (OU) within the Windows Active Directory
Plain text password of Active Directory domain administrator, value is masked in the response
Comma separated list of IPv4 addresses of preferred servers for LDAP client. At most two comma separated IPv4 addresses can be passed.

The Active Directory site the service will limit Domain Controller discovery to
NetBIOS name of the SMB server. This name will be registered as a computer account in the AD and used to mount volumes
A domain user account with permission to create machine accounts

maml:description.maml:para: Updates an Azure NetApp Files (ANF) volume according to the optional modifiers provided.
The Update-AzNetAppFilesVolume cmdlet updates an ANF volume.
The name of the ANF account
A hashtable array which represents the backup object
Specifies whether Cool Access(tiering) is enabled for the volume (default false).

CoolAccessRetrievalPolicy determines the data retrieval behavior from the cool tier to standard storage based on the read pattern for cool access enabled volumes. The possible values for this field are: Default - Data will be pulled from cool tier to standard storage on random reads. This policy is the default. OnRead - All client-driven data read is pulled from cool tier to standard storage on both sequential and random reads. Never - No client-driven data is pulled from cool tier to standard storage.

CoolAccessTieringPolicy determines which cold data blocks are moved to cool tier. The possible values for this field are: Auto - Moves cold user data blocks in both the Snapshot copies and the active file system to the cool tier tier. This policy is the default. SnapshotOnly - Moves user data blocks of the Volume Snapshot copies that are not associated with the active file system to the cool tier.
Specifies the number of days after which data that is not accessed by clients will be tiered (minimum 7, maximum 63).
Default group quota for volume in KiBs. If isDefaultQuotaEnabled is set, the minimum value of 4 KiBs applies.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Default user quota for volume in KiBs. If isDefaultQuotaEnabled is set, the minimum value of 4 KiBs applies.
A hashtable array which represents the export policy
Specifies if default quota is enabled for the volume
The location of the resource
The name of the ANF volume
The name of the ANF pool
A hashtable array which represents the protocol types. You need to create Active Directory connections before creating an SMB/CIFS volume
The resource group of the ANF account
The service level of the ANF volume
Enables access based enumeration share property for SMB Shares. Only applicable for SMB/DualProtocol volume
Enables non browsable property for SMB Shares. Only applicable for SMB/DualProtocol volume
If enabled (true) the volume will contain a read-only .snapshot directory which provides access to each of the volume's snapshots (default to true)
Snapshot Policy ResourceId used to apply a snapshot policy to the volume
A hashtable which represents resource tags
Maximum throughput in Mibps that can be achieved by this volume

UNIX permissions for NFS volume accepted in octal 4 digit format. First digit selects the set user ID(4), set group ID (2) and sticky (1) attributes. Second digit selects permission for the owner of the file: read (4), write (2) and execute (1). Third selects permissions for other users in the same group. the fourth for other users not in the group. 0755 - gives read/write/execute permissions to owner and read/execute to group and other users.
The maximum storage quota allowed for a file system in bytes

command:example.maml:title: --------------- Example 1: Update an ANF volume ---------------
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzNetAppFilesVolume -ResourceGroupName "MyRG" -Location "westus2" -AccountName "MyAnfAccount" -PoolName "MyAnfPool" -Name "MyAnfVolume" -UsageThreshold Size
Location : westus2 Id : /subscriptions/subsId/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/MyAnfAccount/capacityPools/MyAnfPool/volumes/MyAnfVolume Name : MyAnfAccount/MyAnfPool/MyAnfVolume Type : Microsoft.NetApp/netAppAccounts/capacityPools/volumes Tags : FileSystemId : 3e2773a7-2a72-d003-0637-1a8b1fa3eaaf CreationToken : MyAnfVolume ServiceLevel : Premium UsageThreshold : 2199023255552 ProvisioningState : Succeeded SubnetId : /subscriptions/subsId/resourceGroups/MyRG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/MyRG-vnet/subnets/default

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates the ANF volume "MyAnfVolume" with the new UsageThreshold size.
